text:
Avenue Supermarts (DMart) - Q4 FY26 Result Analysis

Q4 Revenue: ₹17,683.86 Cr. Up 18.9% YoY. 
Q4 EBITDA: ₹1,210.53 Cr. Up 26.8% YoY. 
Q4 EBITDA margin: 6.85%. Up 42 bps YoY. 
Q4 PAT to owners: ₹656.59 Cr. Up 19.2% YoY.

Q4 showed operating leverage. EBITDA grew faster than revenue.

EBITDA +26.8% YoY on revenue +18.9%, that 800 bps gap is operating leverage showing up. Purchases grew in line with revenue (+18.9%). Other expenses grew 12.0% YoY, well below revenue. The margin expanded 42 bps YoY to 6.85% in Q4.

For the full year FY26: EBITDA ₹5,187 Cr on revenue ₹68,821 Cr,  7.54% margin, down just 2 bps YoY. Management had guided they would accept a minor margin dip for faster growth. 2 bps is essentially flat. They delivered on that commitment.

85 stores in FY26. Guidance was >50.

DMart added 85 new stores in FY26, taking the total to 500, a milestone. The guidance floor was "not less than 50." They added 70% more than the minimum. Store growth rate: 20.5%, at the upper end of the guided 10–20% range. Revenue grew 15.9% YoY, ahead of store count growth, meaning existing stores are still contributing positively.

The two structural cost headwinds: employee costs and DMart Ready.

Employee costs FY26: ₹1,541.48 Cr, up 32.2% YoY. Revenue grew 15.9%. Employee costs grew at exactly 2x the rate of revenue. Wage inflation for entry-level positions compounded by rapid store expansion headcount. No sign of quick moderation.

Finance costs FY26: ₹142.05 Cr, up 104.5% YoY. 85% of this is lease interest (₹120.76 Cr) under IndAS 116. The owned-store model requires massive upfront capex, ₹4,113.35 Cr in FY26, up 20.2% YoY.

DMart Ready is burning ₹253 Cr of PAT annually.

Subsidiaries (primarily DMart Ready) contributed ₹1,852.71 Cr to consolidated revenue but dragged consolidated PAT by ₹253.44 Cr versus standalone. Standalone PAT FY26: ₹3,223.93 Cr. Consolidated PAT to owners: ₹2,970.49 Cr. Management's timeline is breakeven "within a couple of years." At ₹253 Cr annual burn, that is a meaningful number to watch.

Cash flow: strong operations, structurally negative FCF.

CFO FY26: ₹3,466.74 Cr, up 40.8% YoY. CFO/PAT: 1.17x, high quality. The improvement was driven by trade payables growth as DMart scales its supplier relationships. 

Capex: ₹4,113.35 Cr, FCF: -₹646.61 Cr. The owned-property model generates negative FCF in the expansion phase by design. Despite new commercial paper issuances of ₹965 Cr, D/E stands at just 0.10x, leverage is negligible.

Reported PAT FY26 grew 9.7% YoY. Adjusted for a ₹36.01 Cr one-time tax credit in FY25, normalised growth was 11.2%.

Note : This is not an investment advise.

NETWEB TECH Q4 : MTM losses on payables were offset by hedging gains of ₹5.24 Cr , which are reported under other income. 

For clarity, these gains were added back to EBITDA to reflect true operating performance.

- Negative for consumption sectors  
- Pressure on margins of SMEs  
- Energy cost-sensitive industries affected  
- Watch pricing power of companies

- Impact depends on pass-through ability

GST COLLECTIONS – APRIL FY27 SNAPSHOT

Headline Numbers
- Gross GST ₹2.43 trillion, +8.7% YoY  
- Net GST ₹2.11 trillion, +7.3% YoY  
- Both at record high levels  
- Strong start to fiscal year  

Collection Breakdown
- Domestic ₹1.85 trillion, +4.3% YoY  
- Import-linked ₹0.57 trillion, +25.8%  
- Refunds ₹0.32 trillion, +19.3%  
- Import-led growth dominating  

Key Drivers
- Import GST surged sharply YoY  
- March-end filing boosted collections  
- Year-end sales push visible  
- Cyclical factors played role  

Domestic Weakness
- Net domestic growth only 0.3%  
- Consumption trend still subdued  
- Refund-adjusted numbers weak  
- Demand recovery uneven  

Import Impact
- Higher import values boosted GST  
- Rupee depreciation aiding collections  
- Not purely volume-driven growth  
- External factors influencing data  

Refund Trends
- Domestic refunds sharply higher  
- Import refunds declined YoY  
- Distorting net collection trends  
- Impacts true demand assessment  

Context & Caveats
- GST rate cuts impact base  
- April seasonality inflates numbers  
- Not fully structural growth  
- Requires cautious interpretation  

Expert Insight
- Strong tax administration impact  
- Digital compliance improving  
- Wider tax base emerging  
- Underlying resilience present  

Core Theme
- Strong headline, weak domestic trend  
- Import surge driving growth  
- Cyclical boost masking reality  
- Structural improvement still ongoing  

Final Takeaway
- GST collections robust overall  
- Domestic demand needs monitoring  
- Data requires deeper interpretation

APOLLO HOSPITALS – AI & PREVENTIVE HEALTHCARE VIEW

Core Problem
- Diseases appearing earlier in population  
- 1 in 5 under 30 prediabetic  
- Fatty liver often undetected in tests  
- Diseases progress silently for years  

Why Prevention Failed
- System focused on treatment not prevention  
- Insurance and funding favor acute care  
- Prevention lacked economic incentive  
- Symptoms-based detection too late  

What Is Changing
- Employers prioritizing workforce health  
- High obesity and diabetes prevalence  
- Productivity and cost concerns rising  
- Prevention becoming business necessity  

Execution Gap
- Risk detection not leading to outcomes  
- Lack of continuous engagement  
- Follow-up and accountability missing  
- One-time diagnosis insufficient  

Digital Infrastructure
- Integrated EMR, HIS, PACS systems  
- 7.99 million digital consultations FY25  
- 8.5 million lab bookings enabled  
- Strong connected healthcare ecosystem  

Access Expansion
- Teleconsultation bridging city gaps  
- Specialists accessible in smaller towns  
- Seamless data sharing enabled  
- Personalized care becoming scalable  

Role of AI
- Early risk detection before symptoms  
- Continuous monitoring vs one-time tests  
- AI identifies patterns and signals  
- Doctors drive final clinical decisions  

Key Insight
- Data alone has limited value  
- Clinical oversight remains critical  
- AI plus doctors creates real impact  
- Scalable preventive care now possible  

Core Theme
- Shift from reactive to preventive care  
- AI enabling predictive healthcare model  
- Continuous monitoring over episodic care  
- National-scale preventive system emerging  

Final Takeaway
- Preventive healthcare inflection point  
- AI and digital driving transformation  
- Long-term structural healthcare shift

INDIA POWER GRID STRESS – PRE-SUMMER ALERT

Demand Surge
- Peak demand hit 256 GW in April  
- Rise of over 40 GW in month  
- Still before peak summer demand  
- Demand trajectory sharply rising  

Supply Deficit
- Evening deficit 3.5–3.9 GW  
- Night shortfall up to 5.4 GW  
- Stress visible before peak season  
- Supply unable to match peaks  

Solar Paradox
- Solar generation up 27% YoY  
- Day prices near ₹1.5/unit  
- Night prices near ₹10/unit  
- Sharp day-night supply mismatch  

Structural Issue
- Daytime surplus, evening shortage  
- Solar drop after sunset critical  
- Demand peaks post-sunset hours  
- Grid imbalance widening structurally  

Generation Mix Stress
- 22 GW thermal under maintenance  
- Gas plants underutilized, costly LNG  
- Renewables curtailment in Rajasthan  
- Coal plants running flexibly  

Infrastructure Pressure
- Transformers degrading 2–3x faster  
- Cooling cycles disrupted at night  
- Grid stress becoming structural  
- Maintenance challenges increasing  

Storage Gap
- Only ~201 MW storage contribution  
- BESS and pumped storage negligible  
- Huge gap between need and capacity  
- Storage critical missing link  

Expert View
- Structural mismatch intensifying  
- Solar shifts stress to evenings  
- Thermal acting balancing resource  
- Storage seen as key solution  

Core Theme
- Solar success creating grid imbalance  
- Cheap power day, costly at night  
- Storage deficit now critical issue  
- Peak summer risk still ahead  

Final Takeaway
- Grid stress rising structurally  
- Urgent need for storage solutions  
- Key risk for power ecosystem

- Not a buy/sell recommendation

• ⏸️ All further proceedings on the tax demand notices now stayed until Supreme Court delivers its final verdict on the matter

text:
SANDUR MANGANESE & IRON ORES (SMIORE) : HIGH COURT DISMISSES COMPANY'S WRIT PETITION ON AFFORESTATION DEMAND ⚖️🌳

• ⚖️ Karnataka High Court vide order dated April 30, 2026 dismisses writ petition filed by Sandur Manganese & Iron Ores Ltd (SMIORE)

• 🌳 Company had petitioned to set aside DCF (Deputy Conservator of Forests) communication dated June 20, 2025 demanding compensatory afforestation charges

• 💰 Forest department demand stands at Rs 131.25 crore as cost of compensatory afforestation — plus land to be provided for afforestation

• 📋 Background — Karnataka HC had earlier in April 2025 confirmed validity of the company's Forest Clearances, but DCF subsequently raised the afforestation cost demand

• ✅ Company clarifies it holds valid Mining Leases and Forest Clearances — will continue mining operations without interruption

• 🔄 Company to now take steps to resolve the compensatory afforestation charges and land demand issue through other legal or administrative channels

🔴 Impact: Negative (Court dismissal means Rs 131.25 crore afforestation liability demand remains active — adds financial and operational uncertainty for SMIORE, though mining continues with valid clearances)
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
AUROBINDO PHARMA : CURATEQ BIOLOGICS GETS HEALTH CANADA APPROVAL FOR BEVACIZUMAB BIOSIMILAR 💊🇨🇦

• 🏥 CuraTeQ Biologics Pvt. Ltd., wholly owned subsidiary of Aurobindo Pharma, receives Notice of Compliance (NOC) from Health Canada for Bevqolva — its Bevacizumab biosimilar

• ✅ Health Canada NOC confirms Bevqolva meets all regulatory standards for safety, efficacy & quality — grants formal marketing authorization in Canada

• 🎯 Bevacizumab targets VEGF-A to inhibit tumor blood vessel growth — treats multiple cancers including colorectal and lung cancer — available in 100mg & 400mg formulations

• 🌍 Bevqolva already approved & commercially launched in UK (MHRA approval in 2025) — now Canada added as second major market

• 🔄 Product currently under review by CHMP at European Medicines Agency (EMA) — EU approval could be next catalyst

• 📋 CuraTeQ Biologics has 2 additional biosimilar applications currently under review by Health Canada — more approvals in pipeline

• 💊 Reference product Avastin (Roche/Genentech) — Bevqolva is a biosimilar to this blockbuster cancer drug

🟢 Impact: Positive (Canada market entry for a high-value oncology biosimilar strengthens Aurobindo's global biologics footprint — EU approval could be a significant near-term trigger)

text:
Park Medi World Limited is a rapidly expanding hospital chain focused on delivering affordable, quality healthcare through a highly capital-efficient model. It leverages a cluster-based strategy, brownfield acquisitions, and asset-light O&M arrangements to scale capacity at significantly lower capex per bed than peers, while maintaining clinical standards. The company is also transitioning towards higher-acuity specialties , supported by investments in advanced technologies like robotic surgery, leading to improved realisations and operational efficiency.

A large share of revenue from government healthcare schemes ensures stable patient volumes, while a gradual shift towards private and insurance payors is improving cash flows and reducing working capital intensity. With planned capacity expansion, improving case mix, and benefits from CGHS rate revisions, the company is positioned for steady growth.

CIGNITI TECHNOLOGIES : NCLT APPROVES MERGER WITH COFORGE — FINAL ORDER PRONOUNCED 💻🔀

• ⚖️ NCLT Chandigarh Bench pronounces order on April 29, 2026 sanctioning the Scheme of Amalgamation of Cigniti Technologies Ltd. (Transferor) with Coforge Limited (Transferee) under Sections 230-232 of Companies Act, 2013

• 📅 Appointed Date fixed as April 1, 2025 — all assets, liabilities and employees of Cigniti to vest in Coforge from this date

• 🔄 Share Exchange Ratio: 1 Coforge share (FV ₹2) for every 1 Cigniti share (FV ₹10) | Originally 1:5 ratio, adjusted to 1:1 post Coforge's stock split in March 2025 | Economically equivalent — confirmed by PwC, KPMG, JM Financial & Axis Capital

• ✅ Scheme approved by all stakeholder classes with near-unanimous votes | Cigniti shareholders: 99.95% in value | Coforge shareholders: 100% in value | All creditors: 100%

• 🏛️ Cigniti Technologies shall stand dissolved without winding up upon scheme becoming effective | New Coforge shares to be allotted within 30 days of Effective Date

• 💰 Income Tax Dept. raised objections noting pending tax demands of ~₹28.6 Cr (Cigniti) and ~₹304.7 Cr (Coforge) | NCLT directed Coforge to absorb all tax liabilities of Cigniti | ITD retains right to invoke GAAR

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major IT merger milestone — Cigniti's software testing capabilities fully absorbed into Coforge, strengthening its digital assurance & engineering portfolio)

text:
IIFL FINANCE : ACQUIRES 87.8% STAKE IN XTRACAP FINTECH FOR ₹37.7 Cr 💰📲

• 🤝 IIFL Fintech Pvt. Ltd. (wholly-owned subsidiary of IIFL Finance) approves acquisition of majority stake in Xtracap Fintech India Pvt. Ltd. on April 30, 2026

• 🏗️ Deal Structure — 2 Stage:
Stage I: Secondary acquisition of existing shares for ~₹25.7 Cr
Stage II: Fresh primary capital infusion of ~₹12 Cr via equity subscription

• 📊 Post-deal, IIFL Fintech will hold ~87.8% stake in Xtracap | Earlier held 18.8%

• 🔗 Xtracap's Business: Supply Chain Financing — anchor-led, invoice-linked credit solutions to distributors & retailers across sectors | FY25 turnover: ₹7.03 Cr (scale-up phase)

STERLITE TECHNOLOGIES Q4 FY26 CONCALL UPDATE

Legal Case Update
- Ongoing legal matter with Prysmian Cables discussed in concall  
- Management indicated no immediate operational impact  
- In case of adverse outcome, company has options  
  - Appeal the verdict  
  - Continue legal proceedings  
  - Explore negotiation routes  

Fundraise Clarification
- ₹2,000 Cr mentioned in P&L created confusion  
- Not an immediate fundraise plan  
- Clarified as part of broader financial context, not active capital raising  

Management View
- Addressed investor concerns proactively  
- Legal risks acknowledged but manageable  
- No immediate financial stress from fundraise item  

Final Takeaway
- Legal overhang remains but with multiple fallback options  
- No near-term dilution risk from misunderstood fundraise mention  

Impact
- Neutral

PRIME CABLE BUSINESS OVERVIEW

Business Model
- B2B wires and cables manufacturer focused on T and D segment  
- Operates largely in low voltage segment (1.1kV to 33kV)  
- Supplies to SEBs, EPC contractors and industrial players  

Product Mix
- Control cables 47%  
- Power cables 27%  
- Aerial Bunch Cables 16%  
- Others small share  

Revenue Mix
- Tenders 57%  
- EPC contractors 39%  
- Direct sales 4%  

Client Mix
- Government, PSU and EPC 53.5%  
- Private sector 46.5%  

Value Chain
- Transmission 53.5%  
- Distribution 33.5%  

Order Book
- H1FY26 ₹106 Cr  
- Q3FY26 ₹130 Cr (↑ 95% YoY)  
- Q4FY26 ₹170 Cr  
- Post Apr 2026 ₹217 Cr  

Capacity Expansion
- Current capacity ₹350 Cr  
- Additional ₹150 Cr capacity by Q2FY27  
- New Rajasthan plant operational by Q1FY27  

Regulatory and Product Expansion
- BIS upgrade from 3.3kV to 33kV  
- Solar cable certification (1500V DC)  
- Entry into renewables and higher voltage cables  

Guidance
- FY26 utilization 60 to 65%  
- FY27 utilization above 80%  
- New capacity utilization 30 to 40% in first year  

Q4 FY26 Performance
- Revenue ₹72.3 Cr (↑ 66.5% YoY)  
- FY26 Revenue ₹234.6 Cr (↑ 66.4% YoY)  
- Strong order inflows with ₹32 Cr largest order  

Growth Drivers
- T and D capex expansion  
- Renewable energy push  
- Rising EPC demand pipeline  

Risks
- Tender driven business  
- High working capital requirement  
- Execution dependency  
- Limited retail presence  

Final Takeaway
- High growth SME play benefiting from T and D cycle with strong order book and expansion into higher value segments  

Impact
- Positive

text:
Park Medi World Limited is a rapidly expanding hospital chain focused on delivering affordable, quality healthcare through a highly capital-efficient model. It leverages a cluster-based strategy, brownfield acquisitions, and asset-light O&M arrangements to scale capacity at significantly lower capex per bed than peers, while maintaining clinical standards. The company is also transitioning towards higher-acuity specialties , supported by investments in advanced technologies like robotic surgery, leading to improved realisations and operational efficiency.

A large share of revenue from government healthcare schemes ensures stable patient volumes, while a gradual shift towards private and insurance payors is improving cash flows and reducing working capital intensity. With planned capacity expansion, improving case mix, and benefits from CGHS rate revisions, the company is positioned for steady growth.

COAL INDIA : BAGS LOA FROM GRIDCO FOR 320 MWh BATTERY ENERGY STORAGE SYSTEM (BESS) PROJECT IN ODISHA ⚡🔋

• 📋 Coal India Limited (BSE: 533278) receives Letter of Award (LOA) from GRIDCO Limited, Odisha for setting up a 320 MWh (80 MW x 4 hours) Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) Plant — dated April 30, 2026

• 🏗️ Project details — 2 clusters:
Cluster 1: Padampur & Bolangir — tariff ₹3.04 lakh/MW/Month
Cluster 2: Bhatli & Basta — tariff ₹3.05 lakh/MW/Month

• 💰 Estimated project cost: ~₹400 crores

• ⏱️ Execution timeline: 18 months from signing of BESS Power Agreement (BESPA) | Documents to be submitted within 20 days of LOA + Performance Bank Guarantee (PBG) to be submitted by CIL

• ✅ Domestic order | Not a related party transaction

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic diversification win for Coal India into clean energy storage — BESS project in Odisha marks CIL's growing pivot beyond coal into renewable energy infrastructure; aligns with India's energy transition goals)

text:
VEDANTA LIMITED : CRISIL REAFFIRMS AA/WATCH DEVELOPING RATING; NCD RATING WITHDRAWN POST DEMERGER TRANSFER 🏦📋

• 📋 Vedanta Limited (NSE: VEDL) files credit rating update — CRISIL Ratings reaffirms ratings on April 30, 2026 at 07:50 PM IST

• ⭐ Ratings reaffirmed:
Long-term: CRISIL AA / Watch Developing (unchanged)
Short-term: CRISIL A1+ (reaffirmed)

• 🔄 NCD rating withdrawn: CRISIL withdraws rating on NCDs worth ₹6,089 crores — reason: NCDs being transferred to Vedanta Aluminium Metal Ltd (VAML) as part of Vedanta Group demerger; VAML will operate the aluminium business post-demerger

⚡ Is Module Overcapacity a Real Concern in India? A Deeper Look  

👇At first glance, rising module capacity may suggest oversupply risk. 

But the reality is more nuanced.

🔹 Supply vs Demand Reality
With ALMM 2 in place, India has ~30 GW of integrated cell + module capacity, while demand is already 50–55 GW.

🔹  Future ALMM 3 (2028) will require backward integration into ingot & wafer, further tightening “usable” supply.

👉 Indicates no industry-wide glut, but selective competitiveness.

🔹 Margins Driven by Integration, Not Capacity
 As cell manufacturing ramps up, players achieve better cost control → margin expansion ahead.

📊 Conclusion:
India’s solar market is not facing overcapacity—
It is transitioning to a high-efficiency, integrated manufacturing ecosystem.

The real differentiator going forward:  Backward integration + execution + product mix.

text:
PFC : 5 NEW SPV SUBSIDIARIES INCORPORATED UNDER PFC CONSULTING FOR RENEWABLE ENERGY TRANSMISSION PROJECTS ⚡🔋

• 📋 Power Finance Corporation Ltd. (NSE: PFC) informs that PFC Consulting Limited (PFCCL — WOS of PFC) has incorporated 5 new SPV companies as wholly owned subsidiaries under TBCB (Tariff Based Competitive Bidding) mode — dated April 30, 2026

• 🏗️ 5 SPVs incorporated for developing major RE transmission infrastructure:
1️⃣ Jam Khambhaliya REZ Transmission Ltd. — RE integration in Gujarat Phase-II (5,500 MW) + Jamnagar Phase-I (1,000 MW)
2️⃣ Lakadia A Power Transmission Ltd. — Evacuation from Lakadia Phase-II (7.5GW) + Jam Khambhaliya (5.5GW) + Jamnagar (1GW) — Part A
3️⃣ Lakadia C Power Transmission Ltd. — Same corridors as above — Part C
4️⃣ Ananthpuram Transmission Ltd. — Integration of Ananthpuram-III PS REZ Phase-I (3GW)
5️⃣ Krishnagiri REZ Transmission Ltd. — Integration of Krishnagiri REZ Phase-I

• 🔄 Process: PFCCL as Bid Process Coordinator (BPC) will prepare project profiles, initiate land acquisition & forest clearances — SPVs to be transferred to successful bidder post competitive bidding

• 🌱 Total RE capacity being enabled across all projects: ~22+ GW — aligned with India's National RE transmission buildout

🟢 Impact: Positive (Demonstrates PFC's expanding role in India's RE transmission ecosystem — large-scale infrastructure pipeline being activated through PFCCL's BPC mandate)
channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
VODAFONE IDEA : DoT FINALISES AGR DUES AT ₹64,046 CR — DOWN FROM ₹87,695 CR 📡⚖️

• 📋 Vodafone Idea Limited (NSE: IDEA) files AGR matter update — DoT Committee finalises AGR dues at ₹64,046 crores as on December 31, 2025 — dated April 30, 2026

• 📉 Significant reduction: AGR dues revised DOWN by ₹23,649 crores from the earlier frozen amount of ₹87,695 crores (as intimated on January 27, 2026)

• 💳 Payment schedule finalised:
Phase 1 — Minimum ₹100 crores per year for 4 years (FY2031-32 to FY2034-35)
Phase 2 — Remaining balance in 6 equal annual instalments (FY2035-36 to FY2040-41)

• ⏳ Effectively no AGR cash outflow required until FY2031-32 — giving Vi a ~5 year runway before payments begin

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major relief for Vi — AGR liability reduced by ~₹23,649 Cr & repayment deferred till FY2032 with a very long 10-year payment window; significantly eases near-term financial pressure & improves survival prospects of the company)

INDOCO REMEDIES : BOARD APPROVES SLUMP SALE OF OPHTHALMIC DIVISION TO SUNWAYS (INDIA) FOR ₹110 CR 👁️💊

• 📋 Indoco Remedies Limited (NSE: INDOCO) Board approves Agreement to Transfer Business (ATB) for slump sale of its Ophthalmic Division to Sunways (India) Pvt. Ltd. — Board meeting held April 30, 2026 (5:15 PM – 6:30 PM IST)

• 💰 Sale consideration: ₹110 crores (cash) — subject to closing adjustments as per ATB terms

• 🌍 Territory covered: India + 17 African countries including Kenya, Tanzania, Zambia, Zimbabwe, Namibia, Senegal, Cameroon, Congo, Gabon & others

• 📊 Division contribution: Ophthalmic Division contributed ₹47.79 crores revenue in FY2025 — representing only 3.2% of Company's standalone total revenue from continuing operations

• 🎯 Rationale: Strategic focus on core therapeutic areas — Ophthalmic Division classified as non-core, hence divested

• 🏢 Buyer profile: Sunways (India) Pvt. Ltd., Mumbai-based — FY25 standalone revenue of ₹137.42 crores | Not a related party

• ⏱️ Transaction expected to close in ~3 months, subject to conditions precedent | No change in shareholding pattern

🟢 Impact: Positive (Non-core division divestment at ₹110 Cr — unlocks capital & sharpens strategic focus on core pharma segments; clean slump sale structure with no promoter conflict)

text:
DEEPAK NITRITE : WOS DEEPAK CHEM TECH SIGNS LONG-TERM HYCO PLANT AGREEMENT WITH PRAXAIR INDIA FOR POLYCARBONATE PROJECT 🏭⚗️

• 🤝 Deepak Chem Tech Limited (DCTL) — WOS of Deepak Nitrite Ltd. (NSE: DEEPAKNTR) — enters into long-term agreement with Praxair India (a Linde company) to build a dedicated HyCO plant for its upcoming Polycarbonate manufacturing facility at Padariya, Dahej, Gujarat — dated April 30, 2026

• 🏗️ Under the agreement, Praxair India will build, own & operate a dedicated on-site facility to supply key feedstocks (Hydrogen & Carbon Monoxide) for polycarbonate production — ensuring reliable long-term raw material supply

• 🎯 On-site model designed to support consistent availability, operational efficiency & supply chain resilience — allowing DCTL to focus on its core downstream manufacturing expertise

• 🚗 DCTL's Polycarbonate facility targets high-growth segments: Automotive, Electrical & Electronics, Construction & Consumer Applications in India

• 📅 On-site HyCO facility expected to be commissioned in 2028 — in line with DCTL's Polycarbonate plant commissioning timeline

🟢 Impact: Positive (Critical raw material supply secured via dedicated on-site infrastructure — significantly de-risks execution of DCTL's marquee Polycarbonate project & enhances long-term project visibility)

• 🔥 Fire incident reported at Oncology Quality Control Laboratory of Panacea Biotec Pharma Limited (PBPL) — WOS of Panacea Biotec Ltd. (NSE: PANACEABIO) — at Baddi, Himachal Pradesh on April 30, 2026 at ~12:23 PM

• ⚡ Cause: Short circuit at the Oncology QC Lab

• ✅ Fire controlled by internal team in time — no human casualties or injuries reported

• ⏸️ Oncology Facility operations temporarily disrupted — estimated downtime: 5 to 7 days

• 🛡️ Facility is adequately covered by insurance — intimation to Insurance Company already made

• 💰 Quantum of loss/damage currently being assessed — however, company confirms no material financial impact expected

• 🔧 PBPL taking all necessary measures to restore operations at the earliest

🔴 Impact: Negative (Short-term operational disruption at Oncology facility — temporary setback though insurance coverage & no material financial impact provide some comfort)

FIRSTSOURCE SOLUTIONS : PARTNERS WITH TYPEFACE TO LAUNCH AGENTIC MARKETING SERVICES 🤖📣

• 🤝 Firstsource Solutions Limited (NSE: FSL) announces strategic partnership with Typeface — a leading AI marketing orchestration engine — to launch Agentic Marketing Services, dated April 30, 2026

• 🚀 New offering transforms enterprise marketing operations into a full-stack, AI-native scalable growth engine — delivering measurable top-line revenue growth beyond just operational efficiency

• 📊 Key industry stat: ~30% of CMO budgets go to content; ~55% of content work is manual — while 90% of those manual workflows can be AI-enabled

• 🏦 Combined strengths: Typeface brings AI orchestration & brand intelligence; Firstsource brings deep operational expertise in regulated industries — Banking, Financial Services, Healthcare & Retail

• 🎯 Target outcome: Help enterprises shift from fragmented activity to scalable, revenue-led execution via micro-segmentation, personalization & AI-driven retention engines

• 📈 Enterprises deploying AI-driven personalization are seeing up to 40% higher revenue vs peers

🟢 Impact: Positive (High-value AI partnership expands Firstsource's digital services portfolio — strengthens positioning in the fast-growing agentic AI & marketing tech space)

ACTION CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT (ACE) : TRANSFERS HEAVY CRANES BUSINESS TO JV WITH KATO WORKS, JAPAN 🏗️🤝

• 🇯🇵 ACE Board approves Business Transfer Agreement (BTA) to transfer its Heavy Cranes Business to ACE KATO Private Limited — a 50:50 JV incorporated with KATO Works Co. Ltd., Japan (incorporated March 11, 2026)

• 🏚️ Business transferred via slump sale as a going concern — covering Truck Cranes, Crawler Cranes & Rough Terrain Cranes, including all operations

• 💰 Consideration: Lump sum transfer price to be paid by JV Company to ACE through issue of ACE subscription shares (not cash)

• 📊 Heavy Cranes Business contributed revenue of Rs. 85.77 crore in FY25 — just 2.58% of ACE's total standalone revenue of Rs. 3,320 crore

• 📅 Expected completion of transaction: On or before June 30, 2026

• 🔄 Post-closing: ACE and KATO each to hold 50% stake in the JV Company

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic JV with Japanese crane major — ring-fences Heavy Cranes into a focused entity, unlocks global tech & scale synergies with KATO Works)

- Structural growth driven by healthcare and new semiconductor opportunity with improving margins and strong global positioning

INDIA ELECTRONICS SECTOR – OUTPUT CUTS AMID MEMORY CHIP CRISIS ⚠️📉

THE CRISIS  
• Memory chip shortage disrupting production  
• OEMs & EMS firms cutting output by 10–20%  
• Memory prices up 4–5x in recent quarters  

👉 Supply shock impacting entire electronics ecosystem  

AFFECTED SEGMENTS 📱💻  
• Mobile phones (worst hit)  
• PCs & servers  
• Set-top boxes  
• TVs & consumer electronics  
• Automotive-grade memory also constrained  

VOLUME IMPACT  
• Overall production decline: 10–15%  
• Manufacturing output falling in line  

👉 Broad-based slowdown across categories  

INDUSTRY RESPONSE – “SURVIVAL OF THE PREMIUM” 🧭  
• Shift towards premium, high-ASP products  
• Reduce entry-level production  
• Strategies:  
  • Configuration tweaks  
  • Cost controls  
  • Selective price hikes  

👉 Protecting margins over volumes  

WHAT THIS MEANS  
• Entry-level demand supply will tighten  
• Premium segment gets priority allocation  
• Price increases likely across categories  

CORE INSIGHT ⚡️  
• Semiconductor dependency still a structural risk  

👉 India manufacturing scale rising, but supply chain still global  

FINAL TAKEAWAY  
• Near-term: production cuts + margin pressure  
• Medium-term: premiumisation accelerates  
• Watch:  
  • Memory prices  
  • Supply recovery timeline  

👉 “Volume slowdown, margin defence — classic supply shock playbook.”  

Impact: Negative (short-term) / Mixed (premium segment resilient)

text:
NOMURA ON DR REDDY'S LABS

Maintain Buy with an unchanged target price of ₹1,600

Canada's decision augurs well for approval in other markets

Positive impact on financials - primarily due to the potential for high price realization in the early stage of genericization

From a medium-to-long-term perspective, believe the upside will be on account of a higher market share that the company can establish by virtue of being an early entrant

Expect a starting price of ~$60-90 per pen, which may eventually settle at $20 per pen

For 12mn pens, this could imply an initial market size of ~700 mn-1.0 bn which would eventually settle at ~USD250mn

Estimate company can generate revenues of ~$75mn assuming a 30% sustainable market share

NOMURA ON DR REDDY'S LABS

Biosimilar Abatacept is the next key product opportunity to watch out for

In Europe, believe an approval and launch of both the formulations is possible in mid 2027F

Haven't factored any upside from Abatacept in estimates currently because of uncertainty on the regulatory approval

Think an approval and launch in the US and the EU will have a significant and sustained upside for Dr Reddy's as it may remain the only biosimilar in the market for a long period of time

Abatacept can become a $450mn+ annual opportunity for the company

Think sustained growth in non-US markets, could drive a re-rating in the stock

MOTILAL OSWAL ON CEAT

Maintain Buy with target price of ₹4,228 vs ₹3,943

Spurt in costs to test the industry's pricing discipline

PAT beat estimates on higher other income

GST rate cut has helped boost tyre demand, both in replacement and OEM segments

However, the recent surge in input costs is likely to drive near-term margin pressure, as the industry will need a couple of quarters to pass on the entire impact

Remain positive on the long-term benefits that this acquisition can deliver for the group

**JAIN RESOURCE RECYCLING (JAINREC) – THE "URBAN MINING" COMPOUNDER**

**STRATEGIC PIVOT: FORWARD INTEGRATION IN COPPER**
• **Current Status:** Transitioning from primary recycling (Scrap to Ingots) to high-value-added products.
• **Phased Commissioning (Unit 3):**
  - **Copper Anodes:** Started Feb 2026 (800 MT/month); doubling to 1,600 MT in Q1 FY27.
  - **Copper Cathodes:** Started Mar 2026 (750 MT/month); doubling to 1,500 MT by Q3 FY27.
  - **Downstream:** Wire rods (600 MT) and Busbars (500 MT) expected in Q1 FY27.
• **Margin Impact:** Shift to value-added products is expected to push EBITDA/MT above ₹55,000 (from ~₹46,000 baseline).

**JOINT VENTURES & RAW MATERIAL SECURITY**
• **Jain CY Circular Solutions (Ahmedabad):** JV with US-based C&Y Group to process 72,000 MT of scrap per annum.
  - **Output:** ~25,000 MTPA of processed copper starting June 2026.
  - **Benefit:** Direct access to C&Y’s 11-12 US-based scrap yards, securing high-quality feedstock.
• **Kuwait Expansion:** Acquired 25% stake in a battery recycling plant (2,000 MT/month) to secure Middle East scrap flows, operational by Q3 FY27.

**MULTI-METAL DIVERSIFICATION & "GREENIUM"**
• **Critical Metals:** Tin capacity doubled to 500 MTPA; Antimony extraction (100 MT/month) from lead bullion slated for Q3 FY27.
• **Green Advantage:** "Zero waste, zero burning" philosophy using green fuels allows the company to target premium "Green Metal" procurement contracts from global OEMs like Apple and Siemens.
• **Market Positioning:** One of the few multi-commodity recyclers (Lead, Copper, Aluminum, Tin, Antimony) globally, reducing dependence on any single metal cycle.

**FINANCIAL SNAPSHOT (9M FY26)**
• **Revenue:** ₹6,438 Cr (↑ 38% YoY).
• **EBITDA:** ₹449 Cr (↑ 65% YoY) | Margin: 7.0%.
• **PAT:** ₹281 Cr (↑ 65% YoY).
• **Return Ratios:** ROE at 30.1% | ROCE at 24.9% (Pre-expansion metrics).
• **Valuation:** Trading at ~40-60x P/E, reflecting a significant premium over peers like Gravita and Pondy Oxides due to its aggressive 40-50% growth guidance.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• JRR is effectively an **"L&T of Recycling,"** managing four massive concurrent expansion projects.
• The strategic shift into **Data Center-grade copper products** (Busbars/Anodes) aligns perfectly with the AI infrastructure boom you’ve been tracking.
• The main monitorable is the **Working Capital Cycle** (currently ~82 days); management expects a drawdown to 60-65 days as the new value-added units stabilize.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Execution-led Re-rating)**

**BHARTI AIRTEL (NXTRA) – GOOGLE AI HUB PROJECT**

**PROJECT OVERVIEW: THE 1GW HUB**
• **Location:** Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
• **Scale:** Groundbreaking for India’s first gigawatt-scale AI Hub.
• **Investment:** A cornerstone of Google’s $15 Billion "AI-First India" commitment (2026–2030).
• **Significance:** This is not just a data center; it is a specialized AI compute cluster designed for high-density GPU workloads.

**AIRTEL’S CRITICAL ROLE (NXTRA + ADANICONNEX)**
• **Partnership:** Nxtra by Airtel is collaborating with AdaniConneX to co-develop the campus.
• **Responsibility:** Airtel handles the "Data Center Lifecycle"—from construction and power management to operational connectivity.
• **Infrastructure Lock-in:** Solidifies Airtel’s position as the preferred infrastructure partner for global hyperscalers entering the Indian market.

**INFRASTRUCTURE & CONNECTIVITY STACK**
• **Connectivity:** Deployment of ultra-low latency fiber networks and a next-gen cable landing station to handle massive cross-border AI data flows.
• **Fiber Expansion:** Significant expansion of the America–India fiber corridor to support Google’s global cloud mesh.
• **Energy:** Integration of long-term clean energy solutions, aligning with India’s 500 GW non-fossil fuel target.

**STRATEGIC VALUE CHAIN IMPACT**
• **Hyperscale Leadership:** Nxtra moves from being a colocation provider to a "Strategic Infrastructure Partner" for the world's largest tech company.
• **Revenue Mix:** Diversifies Airtel’s revenue away from pure mobility/telecom toward high-margin, long-term B2B digital infrastructure contracts.
• **AI Readiness:** Positions Nxtra at the forefront of the "Cooling Economy" and high-density power management required for AI chips.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• The Visakhapatnam project is a structural "moat-building" exercise for Airtel. By providing the "land, power, and fiber" for Google’s 1GW hub, Airtel secures a multi-decade annuity revenue stream.
• This partnership highlights the "Convergence of Giants"—where Airtel’s connectivity meets Adani’s power/land and Google’s compute demand.

**Impact: Highly Positive**

**INVESTEC – FOOD DELIVERY & QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**RATINGS & VALUATIONS**
• **Eternal (Zomato):** **Initiate BUY** | Target Price: **₹375**
• **Swiggy:** **Initiate HOLD** | Target Price: **₹310**
• **Top Pick:** Eternal is identified as the fastest-growing large-cap name in the consumer universe by both Revenue and EBITDA CAGR.

**THE EXECUTION GAP**
• **Food Delivery:** Swiggy trails Eternal by **18–24 months** in operational maturity and profitability metrics.
• **Quick Commerce:** The gap is "materially wider," with Swiggy trailing by **several years** compared to Blinkit’s execution and dark-store density.
• **Defensibility:** Food delivery is viewed as a defensible moat for now, providing the cash flow to fund the "Survival of the Fittest" war in Quick Commerce.

**EMERGING OPPORTUNITIES**
• **Asset-Light Play:** Shift toward "Emerging Platforms" (e.g., Eternal’s *District* app for going-out) is seen as a high-potential, low-capital opportunity.
• **Productivity:** Focus is shifting toward platforms that can successfully integrate non-grocery categories to boost Average Order Value (AOV).

**CORE INSIGHT**
• Investec’s preference for Eternal over Swiggy stems from the former's ability to reach a "profitability inflection point" much earlier. 
• While the market is large enough for both, Eternal’s lead in the high-stakes Quick Commerce segment (Blinkit) provides it with a structural valuation premium.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Eternal) | Neutral (Swiggy)**

**MACQUARIE GROUP – QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**INDUSTRY DYNAMICS & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**
• **High Intensity:** Segment-wide competition is expected to remain elevated, fueled by rapid geographic expansion and diversifying product categories.
• **Amazon’s Move:** Amazon Now is scaling to **100 cities** in India, backed by a **$300 Mn** investment commitment. This signals a shift from "optional" to "core" for the global e-commerce giant in the Indian market.
• **Price Wars:** Aggressive scaling by deep-pocketed incumbents is likely to trigger renewed customer acquisition battles and heavy discounting.

**OPERATIONAL & ECONOMIC CONCERNS**
• **Dark-Store Fragility:** Rapid expansion of dark-store networks is outpacing immediate demand growth in some micro-markets, leading to underutilization.
• **Unit Economics:** Pressure on "Take Rates" and rising delivery partner incentives are straining the path to sustainable profitability.
• **Efficiency Hurdles:** Delivery cost efficiency remains the primary bottleneck as traffic congestion and labor costs rise across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities.

**STRATEGIC EXECUTION PRESSURES**
• **Last-Mile Logistics:** The battle is shifting from "who has the product" to "who has the fastest, most reliable delivery fleet."
• **Inventory Risk:** Expanding into higher-margin non-grocery categories (Electronics, Apparel) increases inventory holding risk and returns management complexity.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• While Quick Commerce (QC) has achieved high consumer adoption, the **"Amazon Entry"** at scale shifts the game from a growth race to a capital endurance race.
• Existing players like Zepto, Blinkit, and Swiggy Instamart face a double-edged sword: they must expand to protect territory, but every new dark store temporarily dilutes their margin profile.
• Unit economics will remain under fire until the industry moves toward a higher **Average Order Value (AOV)** by including high-value non-staple goods.

**Impact: Negative (Sectoral Margin Compression)**

• **SMA Levels:** Special Mention Accounts (SMA) have declined sharply, indicating lower future slippage risk.  
• **Slippages:** Under control with better-than-industry recovery trends.  

**FUTURE GUIDANCE & METRICS** • **ROA:** Guided at 1.6% – 1.7%  
• **ROE:** Guided at 13% – 14%  
• **Growth Visibility:** Operating metrics suggest the bank is entering a high-growth phase while maintaining credit discipline.  

**CORE INSIGHT** • City Union Bank has successfully navigated past asset quality hurdles and is now pivoting back to an aggressive growth stance.  
• The sharp decline in SMA levels and the 25.7% loan growth indicate that the bank's MSME-heavy engine is firing on all cylinders again.  
• With ROA approaching the 1.7% mark, the bank is trading at attractive valuations relative to its historical averages.  

**Impact: Highly Positive**
